関東甲信や東北太平洋側は、この時期らしい服装で良さそうです。東北日本海側や北陸はカラッとした暑さ。東海や西日本は少し蒸し暑く、風通しの良い服装が良さそうです。

In the foreground, a pair of combat boots— Docs, obviously—stepping on a shattered police baton. Dust and pollen in the air. High detail, textures like you can feel the grit. It’s beautiful but it screams 'you're next.

images.alt: A hauntingly beautiful surrealist portrait rendered in photorealistic 3D. A female figure with flawless matte blue skin stands in three-quarter view, her gaze tilted upward with an expression of quiet contemplation. Her entire body — face, neck, shoulders, torso, cap and coat — is layered with extraordinarily intricate embroidery-like filigree: densely woven swirling tendrils and branching fractal patterns in warm gold, burnt amber, saffron and deep red-orange, set against the cool blue base. The texture reads simultaneously as fine textile embroidery, coral reef growth, and illuminated manuscript decoration. Her eyes are strikingly real — clear blue irises, natural black lashes. One smooth, unadorned blue hand rests softly against her jaw. The background is a brooding, atmospheric overcast sky in dark teal and slate grey, lending the entire image a melancholic, otherworldly mood. Octane render quality, hyper-detailed surface, cinematic lighting.

images.alt:
A slender synthetic humanoid android launches through a rain-soaked cyberpunk alley during a high-speed combat encounter, pale artificial skin stretched over partially exposed mechanical framework. Dark asymmetrical hair whips violently across one side of the face while exposed servos, wiring, and precision actuators are visible along the scalp, neck, shoulders, and jawline. A single glowing red eye locks onto its target with machine-like precision.

The android wears a flowing black combat dress covered in oversized white kanji calligraphy layered across reflective fabric panels. During movement, the kanji appear to tear loose from the garment and trail through the air as fragmented strokes of luminous ink. Beneath translucent sections of the outfit, glowing red, pink, and blue circuitry pulses visibly through the torso, ribs, arms, and abdomen, revealing the machine operating beneath the human facade.

record.text:
Ancient Roman shipwreck lacquered underwater
#UnderwaterArchaeology #JapaneseLacquerArt #HistoricalFusion #AIArt

images.alt:
A three-dimensional relief artwork mounted on a curved wooden-edged panel, depicting an underwater shipwreck scene. The piece combines painted and sculpted elements to create a richly detailed seabed tableau.

The background features deep teal and blue-green tones with shafts of light filtering down from above, creating an atmospheric underwater ambiance. Schools of silver-grey fish swim in loose formations through the mid-water layers.

Along the sandy ocean floor, numerous ancient clay amphoras and pottery vessels lie scattered, rendered in warm terracotta and rust-brown hues, encrusted with barnacles and sea growth. Pink and orange coral branches emerge between the artifacts. A green verdigris-patinated ship's wheel rests prominently in the center foreground, suggesting an ancient maritime wreck site. Additional fragments of broken pottery, anchor components, and scattered shells complete the seafloor composition.

The artwork skillfully combines painted depth with raised three-dimensional sculpted elements, giving the piece a tactile, layered quality. The glossy surface enhances the illusion of being submerged underwater. The wooden frame edges in warm walnut tones contrast elegantly with the cool oceanic palette of the main scene.

record.text:
Pride Weaving Honors Ancestral Traditions
#Pride #LGBTQ #Rainbow #AIArt

images.alt:
A vibrant woven textile wall hanging displayed on a dark wooden rod with tab loops, mounted against a cream-colored wall near a window. The nearly square tapestry features a bold, symmetrical mandala-like composition with concentric rectangular borders radiating outward from a vivid magenta center.

The central panel showcases an eight-pointed star in deep red, orange, and gold tones, surrounded by small animal or spirit figures. Moving outward, each border layer introduces new symbolic motifs: open handprints, small heart shapes within diamond frames, interlocked rings or circles, and chevron patterns in rainbow progression.

The color palette is intensely saturated, moving from hot pink and orange at the center through golden yellow, emerald green, cobalt blue, and rich purple at the outer edges. This rainbow gradient creates a warm-to-cool transition that draws the eye inward.

The bottom edge finishes with a decorative fringe of alternating blue and gold threads. The overall aesthetic evokes Indigenous or folk textile traditions, incorporating geometric patterns and symbolic iconography suggestive of community, unity, and spiritual connection. The craftsmanship appears intricate and handmade, with visible texture throughout the weave.

images.alt:
COLUMN:
INTERNET RESOURCES
AI LITERACY RESOURCES
Annis Lee Adams
University Libraries, California State University - East Bay, Hayward, CA

We know students are using generative AI to brainstorm research topics, to develop research questions, and to find sources for research projects in addition to using AI for writing papers and creating presentations. We also know that, at this time, AI tools have limited ability to find high-quality, relevant sources; frequently hallucinate citations that do not exist; and provide no grounding or context for why they present sources or where they found them.

Teaching students and our academic colleagues AI literacy is imperative. AI literacy includes discussing the strengths and limits of generative AI tools by explaining how they operate, how they may be biased, and how they are different from traditional search engines, such as Google and library databases. Furthermore, AI literacy includes guiding students to create productive, iterative prompts and evaluating AI outputs. Additionally, students need to understand appropriate, ethical, and responsible uses of AI tools.

This #Technology article examines the potential impacts of reliance on #ArtificialIntelligence and discusses how libraries can lead AI literacy initiatives from a place of neutrality, utilizing existing #InformationLiteracy and #CriticalThinking expertise. Read more at 
doi.org/10.1080/1522...

images.alt:
COLUMN: ECHNOLOGY
Brooke Gross
University Libraries, Western Kentucky University, Bowling Green, KY

AI literacy long term: 
Conversations and implications
While there is a great deal of research and discussion on the efficacy, pitfalls, and various uses of artificial intelligence in academic settings, it seems there is very little emphasis on the long-term consequences of delegating thought-based tasks or how information professionals in particular can engage in meaningful and productive conversations surrounding these risks. This article will examine the potential impacts of increased reliance on artificial intelligence and identify ways in which libraries can lead AI literacy initiatives from a place of neutrality, utilizing existing information literacy and critical thinking expertise.

This article reflects on The Grolier Club's rich history, ever-evolving approach, and how their exhibitions further their mission “to celebrate the art and history of the book.” Read more at doi.org/10.1080/1522...

#SpecialLibraries #SpecialCollections

images.alt:
Simple and sustainable: Maintaining the Merry-Go-Round of exhibitions at The Grolier Club
Shira Belén Buchsbaum
In September 2026, The Grolier Club will open our 1,000th and 1,001st exhibitions since our founding in January 1884. These two exhibitions reflect some of the foundational aspects of The Grolier Club’s exhibition’s program: observing, narrating, and interpreting the history of book making; celebrating practitioners and innovators; and expanding notions on what constitutes the art and history of the book. The oldest and largest bibliophilic membership society in the United States, the Grolier Club is proud that over 142 seasons and three buildings in Manhattan, we have always hosted exhibitions of books and prints open to the public free of charge. This paper reflects on that rich history, our ever-evolving approach, and how we continue to develop exhibitions that further our mission “to celebrate the art and history of the book.”

#Claude's rebuttal, tersely summarized: #AI enables delegating thinking at an unprecedented scale, enabling harms previously rare. Learners need generative struggle to develop intuition. Non experts can attempt tasks they cannot verify, with potentially dangerous outcomes. The #scale makes this new.

#AI is new, but the world has survived #disruption in the past. Canning. Gunpowder. Steam. Cars. Etc. The world changed. #GenerativeAI is not the first means to delegate thinking. Having servants or employees doesn't make the #manager less #intelligent. Now we're all opt-in managers. #genai #framing

#Authentication idea I probably won't pursue. Auth and #AgeVerification sometimes requires a photo of you holding your ID card, but generative #AI #genai can fake those. Video is not inherently better. Can #automate a #challenge-response protocol in a live video session via an IVR ("say banana").
